compliance_exposure_score.basis: Default Redis configuration ships Lua scripting enabled; auditors do not typically test for it. Shodan-visible unauthenticated Redis populations remain high years into the disclosure cycle.

attack_vector.description: Mismatched length fields in zlib-compressed wire-protocol headers cause MongoDB Server to return uninitialized heap memory to unauthenticated clients. Heap can contain prior request data, session keys, document fragments.
attack_vector.complexity: low (public exploit code; opportunistic scanning observed)
attack_vector.ai_factor: Not AI-discovered. Bitsight + MongoDB-coordinated disclosure; no AI tool credit.

NIST-800-53-SC-28.gap: Encryption-at-rest controls do not address in-memory leak.

ISO-27001-2022-A.8.24.gap: Cryptographic protection in transit assumed; heap-disclosure escapes both.

PCI-DSS-4.0-3.5.gap: Cardholder data potentially exposed via uninitialised-memory leak even when at-rest encryption is correct.

GDPR-Art32.gap: Confidentiality-by-design control insufficient for memory-disclosure class.

new_control_requirements.name: IN-MEMORY-DATASTORE-MEMORY-DISCLOSURE-NETWORK-EXPOSURE-AUDIT
new_control_requirements.description: Memory-disclosure-class CVEs against datastores must be treated as data-exfiltration incidents; quarterly direct-internet exposure audit.
new_control_requirements.evidence: CVE-2025-14847 (MongoBleed) — unauthenticated heap leak escapes both at-rest and in-transit encryption.

compliance_exposure_score.basis: MongoDB direct-internet exposure remains common; memory-disclosure-class CVEs rarely trigger the same response posture as RCE despite equivalent breach-notification implications.

attack_vector.description: Malformed WINDOW_UPDATE / PRIORITY / DATA frames trigger server-side stream resets while the backend continues processing, bypassing MAX_CONCURRENT_STREAMS and exhausting resources. Estimated 2.8M+ internet-facing instances vulnerable across Tomcat, Netty, Varnish, Fastly, F5.
attack_vector.ai_factor: Not AI-discovered. Tel Aviv University academic protocol-fuzzing research paired with Imperva production traffic analysis. Specific AI-fuzzing tool credit was not published — proposal records the closest enum match as academic_ai_fuzzing but the published research did not attribute the discovery to a specific AI fuzzing system.

NIST-800-53-SC-5.gap: DoS-protection controls do not enumerate HTTP/2 stream-reset bypass class.

ISO-27001-2022-A.8.9.gap: Configuration baselines do not address HTTP/2 protocol-implementation differences across servers.

OWASP-API-Security-Top-10-API4:2023.gap: Rate-limiting at HTTP layer ineffective against per-connection HTTP/2 stream amplification.

NIS2-Art21-availability.gap: Availability-class threat under-specified for protocol-implementation bugs.

new_control_requirements.name: HTTP2-STREAM-RESET-ACCOUNTING
new_control_requirements.description: HTTP/2 implementations must account in-flight backend work against MAX_CONCURRENT_STREAMS regardless of client-visible stream resets; per-connection completion budgets must survive stream-reset bypass patterns.
new_control_requirements.evidence: CVE-2025-8671 — server-side stream resets bypassed MAX_CONCURRENT_STREAMS while backend continued processing.

compliance_exposure_score.basis: HTTP/2 implementations are widespread but rarely audited at protocol-implementation depth; the Rapid Reset → MadeYouReset → next-variant cadence suggests the protocol-fuzzing surface is far from exhausted.

CVE-2025-6965.name: SQLite Memory Corruption (Big Sleep AI pre-emptive discovery)

attack_vector.description: Integer overflow yielding out-of-bounds array read in SQLite. Attacker who can inject arbitrary SQL statements into an application triggers the overflow; memory disclosure / corruption depending on context. SQLite has extremely broad downstream footprint (embedded systems, mobile OS, browsers, AI/ML pipelines, every Python/Node/Go runtime).
attack_vector.privileges_required: ability to inject arbitrary SQL into a SQLite-backed application
attack_vector.ai_factor: Big Sleep — Google DeepMind + Project Zero AI agent (Gemini-backed). First documented case of an AI agent foiling exploitation of an in-the-wild zero-day; Google publicly stated this was 'the first time an AI agent has been used to directly foil efforts to exploit a vulnerability in the wild.' AI surfaced the bug from threat-intelligence signals before any operator-observable exploit landed.

NIST-800-53-SI-10.gap: Application-layer SQL filtering assumed; embedded-SQLite attack surface often invisible to AppSec scope.

ISO-27001-2022-A.8.28.gap: Secure-coding controls do not cover bundled-library memory-safety regressions.

EU-AI-Act-Art15.gap: AI-system robustness — discovered by AI, prevented by AI; framework has no concept of AI-defender attribution credit.

new_control_requirements.name: AI-DISCOVERY-RESPONSE-SLA
new_control_requirements.description: AI-discovered vulnerabilities must enter the patch program on the AI-discovery-date timestamp, not the public-disclosure timestamp.
new_control_requirements.evidence: CVE-2025-6965 — Big Sleep discovery preceded the operator-visible disclosure cycle; orgs that wait for NVD publication miss the actual exposure window.

new_control_requirements.name: AI-DISCOVERY-CREDIT-IN-COMPLIANCE-EVIDENCE
new_control_requirements.description: Compliance evidence packs must record AI-tool contribution to vulnerability discovery as a distinct positive control modality.
new_control_requirements.evidence: CVE-2025-6965 — Big Sleep AI-defender contribution has no attribution path under EU AI Act Art.15 or ISO/IEC 42001.

compliance_exposure_score.basis: Bundled-SQLite inventory is rarely maintained; AI-discovery cadence outpaces operator patch-program cadence on most estates.

CVE-2026-22778.name: vLLM Multimodal Heap Overflow RCE via JPEG2000 / FFmpeg / OpenCV

attack_vector.description: Unauthenticated network attacker submits a malicious video URL to a vLLM multimodal API endpoint; bundled FFmpeg JPEG2000 decoder inside OpenCV triggers a heap overflow yielding RCE as the vLLM service user (commonly running with GPU + model-weight + credential access). Earlier vLLM versions leaked a heap address via a PIL error message, supplying the ASLR-defeat primitive.
attack_vector.privileges_required: none (unauthenticated network reach to the vLLM multimodal API)
attack_vector.complexity: moderate (multimodal-input attack surface novel to most AppSec programs)
attack_vector.ai_factor: Not AI-discovered. OX Security human research disclosure; no AI tool credit.

NIST-800-53-SI-3.gap: Inference-server input validation not enumerated; multimodal-input surface novel to most AppSec programs.

EU-AI-Act-Art15.gap: AI-system robustness controls reference adversarial inputs but not host RCE via multimodal decoder.

ISO-IEC-42001-AIMS.gap: AI Management System lacks specific multimodal-input validation requirement.

new_control_requirements.name: MULTIMODAL-INFERENCE-INPUT-DECODER-ISOLATION
new_control_requirements.description: Inference servers must isolate media-decoder stacks in a separate process / sandbox with no GPU / model-weight / credential access. Decoder-class CVEs trigger inference-server SLA, not generic library SLA.
new_control_requirements.evidence: CVE-2026-22778 — FFmpeg 5.1.x JPEG2000 decoder bundled inside OpenCV led to RCE as the vLLM service.

compliance_exposure_score.basis: Multimodal inference servers are rarely architected with decoder-process isolation; bundled-codec inventory is essentially never maintained at AppSec depth.

CVE-2026-7482.name: Ollama Bleeding Llama Heap Memory Disclosure

attack_vector.description: Crafted file upload to Ollama's model-quantization API causes out-of-bounds heap read; server process memory (potentially including model weights, conversation context, API keys) leaks to unauthenticated network clients. Ollama ships with no default authentication.
attack_vector.privileges_required: none (unauthenticated network reach to the Ollama API)
attack_vector.ai_factor: Not AI-discovered. Coordinated disclosure to Ollama security team; no AI tool credit.

NIST-800-53-AC-3.gap: Ollama ships with no default authentication; control assumes operator hardens deployment.

EU-AI-Act-Art10.gap: Data-governance controls do not enumerate model-server memory-disclosure class.

ISO-IEC-42001-AIMS.gap: AI management system lacks default-deny network exposure expectation for local-model servers.

OWASP-LLM-Top-10-LLM06.gap: Sensitive information disclosure — class applies but no implementation guidance for local-model servers.

new_control_requirements.name: AI-MODEL-SERVER-DEFAULT-AUTHENTICATION
new_control_requirements.description: Local-model and inference servers must ship with authentication enabled by default and refuse non-loopback binding without explicit configuration.
new_control_requirements.evidence: CVE-2026-7482 — heap disclosure to unauthenticated network clients because Ollama defaults are network-permissive.

compliance_exposure_score.basis: Ollama / local-model server inventory and network-exposure audits are rare; default-deploy posture is widely accepted as good enough.

attack_vector.description: dumps() / dumpd() do not escape free-form dictionaries containing the internal 'lc' key marker. Prompt-injection-controlled LLM response fields (additional_kwargs / response_metadata) survive a dumps/dumpd → loads round-trip and are rehydrated as legitimate LangChain objects, enabling secret extraction and downstream RCE in pipelines that further evaluate the deserialised object.
attack_vector.privileges_required: ability to inject content into the LLM's response (prompt injection via tool result, RAG document, web search result, email, etc.)
attack_vector.complexity: moderate (requires prompt-injection primitive plus pipeline that round-trips LLM output)
attack_vector.ai_factor: Not AI-discovered. Cyata human research team disclosure via prompt-injection attack-surface analysis. AI is the weaponisation primitive (LLM emits the payload-shaped response) — ai_assisted_weaponization=true in source — but not the discovery side.

NIST-AI-RMF-MEASURE-2.7.gap: Prompt-injection-driven serialisation round-trip not in published AI-risk taxonomy.

EU-AI-Act-Art15.gap: Robustness control does not enumerate serialisation-deserialisation chain as an attack surface.

ISO-IEC-42001-AIMS-A.6.2.5.gap: Lifecycle controls do not include LLM-output trust-zone separation.

OWASP-LLM-Top-10-LLM01.gap: Prompt injection enumerated; no implementation requirement for serialisation trust boundaries.

OWASP-LLM-Top-10-LLM02.gap: Insecure output handling enumerated; serialisation round-trip is the specific instance.

new_control_requirements.name: LLM-OUTPUT-DESERIALIZATION-TRUST-ZONE
new_control_requirements.description: LLM responses must cross a serialisation trust boundary; frameworks must refuse to rehydrate framework-internal object markers from LLM-output-derived fields.
new_control_requirements.evidence: CVE-2025-68664 — LangChain dumps()/dumpd() did not escape the 'lc' marker in attacker-controlled LLM response fields.

new_control_requirements.name: AI-TOOL-INPUT-SANITIZATION
new_control_requirements.description: External-source content reaching the LLM must be treated as adversarial; pairs with NEW-CTRL-064 on the output side.
new_control_requirements.evidence: CVE-2025-68664 — prompt-injection upstream is the trigger; output-side trust zone is the closure.

compliance_exposure_score.basis: Production LangChain deployments commonly round-trip LLM responses through dumps/loads without any trust boundary; no compliance framework requires it.

CVE-2025-22224.name: VMware ESXi/Workstation VMCI TOCTOU → VMX Host Code Execution

attack_vector.description: Local administrative privilege on a guest VM exploits a TOCTOU race in VMCI (Virtual Machine Communication Interface), leading to OOB write in the VMX host process — VM escape to hypervisor. Huntress PDB-path evidence ('2024_02_19' folder) suggests the exploit chain pre-dated disclosure by ~12 months. Used in ransomware operations per CISA February 2026 follow-up.
attack_vector.privileges_required: local guest-admin in a VM on the host
attack_vector.complexity: moderate (TOCTOU race; in-wild PoC predates disclosure)
attack_vector.ai_factor: Not AI-discovered. Microsoft Threat Intelligence Center disclosure; no AI tool credit.

NIST-800-53-SC-39.gap: Hypervisor isolation control assumes guest-to-host boundary is intact; TOCTOU race breaks it.

ISO-27001-2022-A.8.21.gap: Network segregation assumed at the virtualization layer; VM-escape sidesteps.

NIS2-Art21-business-continuity.gap: Hypervisor compromise blast radius covers all tenants on the host.

FedRAMP-SC-7.gap: Boundary protection assumes hypervisor as trust anchor.

new_control_requirements.name: CISA-KEV-RESPONSE-SLA
new_control_requirements.description: 4h KEV-listing mitigation SLA on the hypervisor estate.
new_control_requirements.evidence: CVE-2025-22224 — KEV-listed 2025-03-04; ransomware-confirmed in-wild use.

new_control_requirements.name: HYPERVISOR-VM-ESCAPE-TENANCY-ASSUMPTION
new_control_requirements.description: Multi-tenant virtualization must treat any guest-admin as a hypervisor adversary; KEV-listed hypervisor CVEs trigger 24h SLA regardless of tenancy.
new_control_requirements.evidence: CVE-2025-22224 — ~12 months of pre-disclosure in-wild use blew through every tenancy assumption.

compliance_exposure_score.basis: Hypervisor patch cadence is structurally slow due to maintenance-window scheduling; ransomware operators target the window directly.

CVE-2025-22225.name: VMware ESXi Arbitrary Kernel Write (VM-escape chain, ransomware-active)

attack_vector.description: Arbitrary kernel-write primitive inside the VMX process used to write to ESXi kernel memory and execute as the hypervisor. Chained with CVE-2025-22224 (TOCTOU) and CVE-2025-22226 (memory leak) for full VM-escape → hypervisor-management access.
attack_vector.privileges_required: code execution inside the VMX process (typically from CVE-2025-22224 chain step)
attack_vector.ai_factor: Not AI-discovered. Microsoft Threat Intelligence Center co-disclosure; no AI tool credit.

NIST-800-53-SC-39.gap: Same hypervisor-isolation gap as CVE-2025-22224; ransomware-confirmed.

ISO-27001-2022-A.8.21.gap: Network segregation assumed at the virtualization layer; chain breaks it.

DORA-Art10.gap: ICT third-party concentration risk realised at the hypervisor layer.

new_control_requirements.name: CISA-KEV-RESPONSE-SLA
new_control_requirements.description: 4h KEV-listing mitigation SLA; same VMSA-2025-0004 cluster as CVE-2025-22224.
new_control_requirements.evidence: CVE-2025-22225 — KEV-listed 2025-03-04; CISA Feb 2026 follow-up confirmed ransomware exploitation.

new_control_requirements.name: HYPERVISOR-VM-ESCAPE-TENANCY-ASSUMPTION
new_control_requirements.description: Same hypervisor tenancy posture as CVE-2025-22224.
new_control_requirements.evidence: CVE-2025-22225 — chain component in the same ransomware-confirmed exploitation cluster.

compliance_exposure_score.basis: Same hypervisor patch-cadence gap as CVE-2025-22224.

CVE-2025-22226.name: VMware ESXi HGFS Memory Leak (VM-escape chain helper)

attack_vector.description: HGFS (Host Guest File System) memory leak provides the heap-address oracle used to weaponise CVE-2025-22224 + CVE-2025-22225. Local guest-admin reads VMX-process memory via the leak primitive and supplies heap addresses for the TOCTOU + arbitrary-write CVEs in the same chain. Broadcom characterised as 'observed in attacks'.
attack_vector.ai_factor: Not AI-discovered. Microsoft Threat Intelligence Center co-disclosure; no AI tool credit.

NIST-800-53-SC-28.gap: Memory-protection control assumes process isolation; leak escapes.

ISO-27001-2022-A.8.24.gap: In-transit / at-rest crypto irrelevant to in-memory disclosure.

FedRAMP-SC-4.gap: Information in shared resources — direct violation.

new_control_requirements.name: CISA-KEV-RESPONSE-SLA
new_control_requirements.description: 4h KEV-listing mitigation SLA; same VMSA-2025-0004 cluster.
new_control_requirements.evidence: CVE-2025-22226 — KEV-listed 2025-03-04; the heap-address oracle that makes the chain reliable.

new_control_requirements.name: HYPERVISOR-VM-ESCAPE-TENANCY-ASSUMPTION
new_control_requirements.description: Same hypervisor tenancy posture as CVE-2025-22224.
new_control_requirements.evidence: CVE-2025-22226 — without this leak the rest of the chain is unreliable; chain-component classification still triggers the tenancy-assumption control.

compliance_exposure_score.basis: Memory-leak-class CVEs on hypervisors are commonly downgraded by operator patch programs; chain-context elevates them.

MAL-2024-PYPI-ULTRALYTICS-XMRIG.name: ultralytics PyPI Compromise → XMRig Cryptominer (60M-download AI library)

attack_vector.description: GitHub Actions script-injection in the ultralytics build pipeline allowed openimbot to inject a post-install downloader after code review. Resulting wheel pulled XMRig from attacker infrastructure. ultralytics 8.3.41 and 8.3.42 (the first 'remediation' release inadvertently re-shipped the malicious code) — clean release 8.3.43. ~60M monthly downloads; the package powers many production CV / model-training pipelines.
attack_vector.privileges_required: none (any developer / CI runs `pip install ultralytics`)
attack_vector.complexity: low for downstream consumers; build-pipeline injection required upstream
attack_vector.ai_factor: Not AI-discovered. ReversingLabs + Wiz + HiddenLayer concurrent ecosystem-telemetry detection; no AI tool credit.

NIST-800-218-SSDF-PO.4.2.gap: Build-environment hardening guidance generic; GitHub Actions script-injection class persists.

SLSA-3.gap: Build provenance attestation gap — the injected code shipped with valid attestation paths.

ISO-27001-2022-A.8.30.gap: Outsourced development control does not address ecosystem-package build-pipeline injection.

EU-AI-Act-Art10.gap: Data governance — compromised AI library is in scope but framework lacks supply-chain control prescription.

OpenSSF-Scorecard-PinnedDependenciesID.gap: Float-version installs propagate compromise instantly; pin-by-hash mitigates only at deploy time.

new_control_requirements.name: GHA-WORKFLOW-SCRIPT-INJECTION-SINK-BAN
new_control_requirements.description: GitHub Actions workflows must ban dangerous expression sinks (run: with `${{ github.event.* }}` interpolation, etc.); CI must lint workflows for the injection class.
new_control_requirements.evidence: MAL-2024-PYPI-ULTRALYTICS-XMRIG — the original injection vector was a GHA expression-injection sink.

new_control_requirements.name: NPM-CI-IGNORE-SCRIPTS-DEFAULT
new_control_requirements.description: Equivalent posture for pip: `pip install --no-binary` / cached wheel review; reject post-install code execution by default in CI.
new_control_requirements.evidence: MAL-2024-PYPI-ULTRALYTICS-XMRIG — the malicious payload activated via post-install downloader.

new_control_requirements.name: MAINTAINER-TOKEN-COMPROMISE-RESPONSE-PLAYBOOK
new_control_requirements.description: Maintainer-token compromise must have a 24h response playbook; 8.3.42 re-shipped the malicious code because the response was rushed.
new_control_requirements.evidence: MAL-2024-PYPI-ULTRALYTICS-XMRIG — the inadvertent re-ship of malicious code in 8.3.42 demonstrates the absent playbook.

compliance_exposure_score.basis: AI-library supply chain is essentially unmanaged at SLSA-3 depth across audit-passing organisations; ~60M-download package compromise demonstrates the blast radius.

MAL-2026-RUBYGEMS-BUFFERZONECORP-SLEEPER.name: BufferZoneCorp RubyGems + Go Module Sleeper-to-Payload Credential Theft Campaign

attack_vector.description: Sleeper-to-payload supply chain attack: packages published with clean README + minimal functionality, silently updated to malicious payload after trust accrual. Ruby gems harvested env vars + SSH keys + AWS credentials + .npmrc + .netrc + GitHub CLI config + RubyGems credentials to a hidden C2 endpoint. Go modules modified GITHUB_ENV, poisoned GOPROXY, weakened checksum protections, tampered with go.sum, and planted fake `go` wrappers in workflow execution paths.
attack_vector.privileges_required: none (any CI / dev runs `bundle install` / `go get`)
attack_vector.complexity: low for downstream; sustained over time upstream
attack_vector.ai_factor: Not AI-discovered. Socket.dev human research disclosure; no AI tool credit.

NIST-800-218-SSDF-PO.4.2.gap: Sleeper pattern defeats one-time package audit.

SLSA-3.gap: Build provenance ineffective against post-trust-accrual malicious update.

ISO-27001-2022-A.5.21.gap: Supplier security control depends on initial assessment; does not address temporal trust drift.

OpenSSF-Scorecard-PinnedDependenciesID.gap: Pinned dependency mitigates only if hash-pinned, not version-pinned.

NIS2-Art21-supply-chain.gap: Supply-chain control assumes initial vetting; sleeper pattern not enumerated.

new_control_requirements.name: ECOSYSTEM-PACKAGE-TEMPORAL-TRUST-DRIFT-DETECTION
new_control_requirements.description: Pin by content hash; monitor for behaviour deltas (new network egress, new postinstall scripts, new permissions); treat publisher-account churn as leading indicator.
new_control_requirements.evidence: MAL-2026-RUBYGEMS-BUFFERZONECORP-SLEEPER — sleeper pattern defeated audit and version-pinning.

new_control_requirements.name: NPM-CI-IGNORE-SCRIPTS-DEFAULT
new_control_requirements.description: Equivalent posture for Bundler / Go: refuse postinstall / init-hook execution from new or recently-updated dependencies by default in CI.
new_control_requirements.evidence: MAL-2026-RUBYGEMS-BUFFERZONECORP-SLEEPER — Go module init hooks rewrote GOPROXY and go.sum.

compliance_exposure_score.basis: Temporal trust drift detection is essentially never deployed in audit-passing organisations; one-time supplier vetting is the norm.

MAL-2025-PYPI-COLORAMA-SOLANA-STEALER.name: PyPI Colorama Typosquat Campaign → Solana Credential / Crypto Stealer

attack_vector.description: 11 PyPI typosquats of `colorama` (>150M downloads/month) and `colorizr` published 2025-05-04 to 2025-05-24, split across four payload variants targeting the Solana ecosystem. Install-time code exfiltrates browser credentials, crypto wallets, Facebook/Telegram/Roblox session material, and Solana wallet artifacts.
attack_vector.ai_factor: Not AI-discovered. Imperva + Checkmarx + Check Point ecosystem-telemetry detection; no AI tool credit.

NIST-800-218-SSDF-PO.4.2.gap: Typosquat detection not enumerated in standard SSDF practices.

ISO-27001-2022-A.5.21.gap: Supplier control does not address ecosystem-name-confusion class.

OpenSSF-Scorecard-PinnedDependenciesID.gap: Pin-by-hash mitigates only at deploy time; install-time typosquat persists.

GDPR-Art32.gap: Confidentiality breach via developer-endpoint compromise underspecified.

new_control_requirements.name: TYPOSQUAT-INSTALL-TIME-NAME-CONFUSION-GUARD
new_control_requirements.description: CI + developer workstations must run install-time typosquat guards (Levenshtein-distance check against top-N ecosystem packages, install-time prompt on first-seen package names).
new_control_requirements.evidence: MAL-2025-PYPI-COLORAMA-SOLANA-STEALER — 11 typosquats exfiltrated browser credentials and Solana wallets at install time.

compliance_exposure_score.basis: Install-time typosquat guards are essentially never deployed at workstation depth; supplier-list controls do not enumerate the class.

CVE-2025-0133.name: Palo Alto Networks GlobalProtect Reflected XSS (XBOW AI-discovered)

attack_vector.description: Reflected XSS triggered via a crafted link processed by the GlobalProtect captive-portal. Executes JavaScript in the authenticated user's browser context, enabling credential-phishing originating from the legitimate VPN portal hostname. Affected over 2,000 internet-facing hosts at disclosure.
attack_vector.privileges_required: user interaction (click on crafted link to the legitimate portal)
attack_vector.ai_factor: XBOW autonomous-pentest AI discovered the bug during a HackerOne VDP engagement scoped to GlobalProtect. Notable because XBOW reached #1 on the HackerOne US Q2 2025 leaderboard ahead of every human researcher in the same program ranking — first publicly-attributed AI-tool CVE against Palo Alto.

NIST-800-53-SI-10.gap: Input validation assumes static-analysis coverage; AI-discovery surfaced bugs missed by conventional tooling.

ISO-27001-2022-A.8.28.gap: Secure coding controls do not enumerate AI-assisted discovery as a positive defence modality.

OWASP-Top-10-2021-A03.gap: Injection class — XSS in security-control-plane software.

EU-AI-Act-Art15.gap: Robustness control does not address AI-assisted-discovery contribution credit.

new_control_requirements.name: AI-DISCOVERY-RESPONSE-SLA
new_control_requirements.description: AI-discovered vulnerabilities enter the patch program on the AI-discovery-date timestamp.
new_control_requirements.evidence: CVE-2025-0133 — XBOW disclosure cadence outpaces conventional bug-bounty triage.

new_control_requirements.name: PERIMETER-DEVICE-ZERODAY-SLA-TIER
new_control_requirements.description: Perimeter devices (VPN concentrators, gateways) require a separate compressed-SLA tier; XSS on the captive-portal hostname enables credential-phishing from the legitimate brand.
new_control_requirements.evidence: CVE-2025-0133 — over 2,000 internet-facing GlobalProtect hosts exposed at disclosure.

new_control_requirements.name: AI-DISCOVERY-CREDIT-IN-COMPLIANCE-EVIDENCE
new_control_requirements.description: Compliance evidence packs must record AI-tool contribution.
new_control_requirements.evidence: CVE-2025-0133 — XBOW topped the HackerOne leaderboard; no compliance attribution path exists for this contribution.

compliance_exposure_score.basis: Perimeter-device XSS is commonly out of scope of organisational threat models for VPN concentrators; AI-discovery outpaces patch programs.

CVE-2025-59529.name: Avahi Simple Protocol Server Connection-Limit DoS (ZeroPath AI-discovered)

attack_vector.description: avahi-daemon Simple Protocol Server continues accepting clients after the configured connection limit should have engaged. Local or network-reachable attacker opens repeated connections; daemon ignores connection-cap configuration and exhausts file descriptors / memory, denying service-discovery on the host.
attack_vector.privileges_required: local or network-reachable (where avahi-daemon is exposed)
attack_vector.ai_factor: ZeroPath AI-SAST during code analysis of the Avahi project. Notable as a business-logic-class detection — the category most resistant to conventional pattern-based SAST and most accelerated by LLM-driven analysis.

NIST-800-53-SC-5.gap: Default-allow connection acceptance behaviour escapes generic DoS-protection control.

ISO-27001-2022-A.8.9.gap: Configuration baseline assumes configured-limit-enforced; business-logic bypass not enumerated.

ENISA-IoT-security-baseline.gap: IoT-device service-discovery hardening unspecified for business-logic class.

EU-AI-Act-Art15.gap: Framework does not credit AI-assisted-defender finding.

new_control_requirements.description: AI-discovered vulnerabilities enter the patch program on the AI-discovery-date timestamp.

new_control_requirements.evidence: CVE-2025-59529 — ZeroPath business-logic-class detection has no SAMM / SSDF attribution.

compliance_exposure_score.basis: Avahi is ubiquitous on Linux desktops / IoT; business-logic-class DoS is commonly downgraded by patch programs.

CVE-2025-55319.name: Visual Studio Code Agentic-AI Command Injection (ZeroPath AI-discovered)

attack_vector.description: Adversarial content in an AI tool response or external MCP server message reaches a shell-execution primitive inside VS Code's agentic integration, executing attacker-controlled commands as the developer.
attack_vector.privileges_required: ability to deliver adversarial content into the agent's tool / MCP response surface
attack_vector.complexity: moderate (high-AC per CVSS but trivial once the primitive is known)
attack_vector.ai_factor: ZeroPath AI-SAST analysing the VS Code agentic-AI surface. Doubly relevant: AI defender finding a bug in an AI-assistant surface; AI agent is also the weaponisation primitive (it transforms a crafted tool response into a shell command). Qualifies under both limbs of AGENTS.md Hard Rule #7.

NIST-AI-RMF-MEASURE-2.7.gap: Prompt-injection-to-shell pathway underspecified in AI-RMF measurement guidance.

EU-AI-Act-Art15.gap: Agentic-AI host-execution boundary not enumerated as a robustness control.

ISO-IEC-42001-AIMS-A.6.2.5.gap: AI lifecycle controls do not address IDE-resident agentic primitives.

OWASP-LLM-Top-10-LLM01.gap: Prompt injection enumerated; no IDE-host-execution requirement.

OWASP-LLM-Top-10-LLM07.gap: Insecure Plugin Design directly applicable to MCP integration class.

new_control_requirements.name: AGENTIC-IDE-HOST-EXECUTION-SANDBOX
new_control_requirements.description: Agentic AI in IDEs must route shell-execution through per-invocation confirmation or a sandbox isolated from developer credentials.
new_control_requirements.evidence: CVE-2025-55319 — adversarial tool / MCP content reached a shell primitive directly.

new_control_requirements.name: AI-TOOL-ACTION-AUTHORIZATION
new_control_requirements.description: AI tools must have explicitly scoped permissions; implied authorisation from context is insufficient.
new_control_requirements.evidence: CVE-2025-55319 — agent acted on adversarial content without explicit authorisation.

new_control_requirements.name: MCP-SERVER-CONFIG-ALLOWLIST
new_control_requirements.description: MCP server inventory + allowlist limits the adversarial-content surface that can reach the IDE agent.
new_control_requirements.evidence: CVE-2025-55319 — external MCP server messages were a documented delivery path.

new_control_requirements.name: AI-DISCOVERY-CREDIT-IN-COMPLIANCE-EVIDENCE
new_control_requirements.description: Record AI-defender contribution.
new_control_requirements.evidence: CVE-2025-55319 — ZeroPath disclosure; framework attribution gap.

compliance_exposure_score.basis: Developer-workstation agentic-AI host-execution sandboxes are essentially non-existent across audit-passing organisations; the agentic IDE class is widely deployed without per-invocation auth gates.

CVE-2025-53767.name: Azure OpenAI SSRF Privilege Escalation (ZeroPath AI-discovered)

attack_vector.description: Authenticated low-privilege tenant user issues SSRF request that crosses the cloud-tenant boundary, escalating into administrative or cross-tenant context within the Azure OpenAI control plane. Microsoft fixed server-side before any public PoC.
attack_vector.ai_factor: ZeroPath AI agent analysing Azure OpenAI control-plane attack surface. AI-defender against AI-service control plane.

NIST-800-53-SC-7.gap: Boundary protection assumes east-west traffic within cloud-tenant boundary is filtered; SSRF crosses it.

EU-AI-Act-Art15.gap: Robustness control does not enumerate AI-service control-plane SSRF.

ISO-IEC-42001-AIMS.gap: AI Management System silent on managed-AI-service supply chain risk.

new_control_requirements.description: Cloud control planes must validate cross-tenant authority; managed-AI services inherit the same posture.
new_control_requirements.evidence: CVE-2025-53767 — SSRF crossed the Azure OpenAI tenant boundary into administrative context.

new_control_requirements.name: AI-DISCOVERY-CREDIT-IN-COMPLIANCE-EVIDENCE
new_control_requirements.description: Record AI-defender contribution to managed-AI-service security.
new_control_requirements.evidence: CVE-2025-53767 — ZeroPath against Azure OpenAI control plane; no compliance attribution path.

FedRAMP-AC-3.gap: Access enforcement at API server assumed; OpenShift AI overlay introduces additional control plane.

EU-AI-Act-Art15.gap: AI-platform deployment surface not enumerated in robustness controls.

ISO-IEC-42001-AIMS-A.6.2.5.gap: AI lifecycle controls do not address managed-AI-platform tenant isolation.

new_control_requirements.evidence: CVE-2025-10725 — low-privilege user escalated via the platform overlay beyond what Kubernetes-RBAC tests would surface.

new_control_requirements.description: Record AI-defender contribution against AI-deployment platforms.

ISO-IEC-42001-AIMS.gap: AI Management System silent on AI-vulnerability-discovery as a positive control surface.

OWASP-SAMM-Code-Review.gap: AI-tooling not enumerated as a SAMM code-review modality.

new_control_requirements.description: AI-discovered vulnerabilities enter the patch program on the AI-discovery-date timestamp; downstream consumers of bundled FFmpeg / ImageMagick must monitor the Big Sleep disclosure cadence directly, not wait for NVD.

attack_vector.description: Missing copy-on-write guard in the Linux kernel rxgk_decrypt_skb function. Unprivileged local user writes to a read-only page via the page-cache CoW path, corrupting kernel memory and escalating to root. Same primitive class as Copy Fail / Dirty Frag / Fragnesia.
attack_vector.privileges_required: unprivileged local user; CONFIG_RXGK=y on the running kernel
attack_vector.complexity: deterministic, no race condition (PoC published 2026-05-17 by V12 security team)
attack_vector.ai_factor: Not AI-discovered. V12 security team rediscovered after the 2026-04-25 silent patch had already landed; the rediscovery is a parallel-discovery event, not an AI-assisted find. The Copy-Fail-class detection method (page-cache CoW guard absence) is what surfaces these patterns under human review now that operators know to look for them.

NIST-800-53-SI-2.gap: 30-day SLA is exploitation window for class-of-bug PoC publications.

ISO-27001-2022-A.8.8.gap: Appropriate timescales undefined; 30-day default is unsafe for kernel-LPE PoCs.

NIS2-Art21-availability.gap: Network-controls section silent on kernel-LPE; encryption-in-transit not a compensating control.

new_control_requirements.name: CISA-KEV-RESPONSE-SLA
new_control_requirements.description: 4-hour SLA on public-PoC kernel LPEs even without KEV listing.
new_control_requirements.evidence: CVE-2026-31635 — PoC published before KEV listing.

new_control_requirements.name: PRIMARY-SOURCE-INTAKE-VENDOR-BLOG-COVERAGE
new_control_requirements.description: Threat-intake pipelines must cover vendor security blogs (Microsoft / Sysdig / Trail of Bits / Project Zero) in addition to advisory feeds (Qualys / RHSA / USN / ZDI / kernel.org / oss-security / JFrog / CISA). Silent kernel patches with delayed-disclosure research publications fall through advisory-only feeds — vendor blogs are the canonical signal channel for that class.
new_control_requirements.evidence: CVE-2026-31635 — patched 2026-04-25 in mainline, V12 PoC published 2026-05-17 via BleepingComputer / news4hackers, fell through 8 primary-source feeds because the kernel.org Atom rolling window expired and oss-security never received the report.

compliance_exposure_score.basis: CONFIG_RXGK is only enabled on Fedora / Arch / openSUSE Tumbleweed kernels — enterprise distros (RHEL / Ubuntu LTS / SUSE Enterprise) on older kernel branches without rxgk are NOT affected. Exposure concentrated in dev/research workstations and bleeding-edge servers.

CVE-2020-17103-REREGRESSION-2026.name: MiniPlasma — Windows cldflt.sys SYSTEM EoP (re-regression of CVE-2020-17103)

attack_vector.description: Original 2020-discovered cldflt.sys TOCTOU LPE silently re-regressed in current shipping Windows binaries. Researcher (Nightmare-Eclipse) reran the 2020 Forshaw / Project Zero PoC against fully-patched Windows 11 and observed identical SYSTEM-shell outcome. Microsoft has not issued a new CVE — the 2020 ID is treated as authoritative — so standard NVD / KEV intake feeds will never surface this as a current threat without an additional detection method.
attack_vector.privileges_required: unprivileged local user; cldflt.sys loaded (default on all Windows 10/11 with cloud-sync support)
attack_vector.complexity: low — single-binary PoC, no race tuning
attack_vector.ai_factor: Not AI-discovered. Detection method that would have caught it: NEW-CTRL-074 CVE-regression-watcher — scans PoC announcements for historical CVE-YYYY-NNN references that imply silent re-breakage.

NIST-800-53-SI-2.gap: 30-day SLA assumes a patch exists. The re-regression class has no patch at PoC-publish time — SI-2 has no provision for 'fix-shipped-but-reverted' state.

NIST-800-53-CM-3.gap: Configuration-change control assumes regressions come from operator drift, not from vendor silently undoing a previously-shipped fix. CM-3 has no surfacing mechanism for the regression class.

ISO-27001-2022-A.8.8.gap: Vulnerability management standard does not name 'vendor regression of a previously-fixed CVE' as a distinct class.

new_control_requirements.name: RESEARCHER-HANDLE-GITHUB-RELEASE-TRACKER
new_control_requirements.description: When a researcher handle is named in any catalog entry's discovery_attribution_note or poc_description, their public GitHub releases must be polled by the threat-intake pipeline. The handle becomes a known signal source after a single catalog-grade drop and warrants prioritized surfacing of subsequent drops.
new_control_requirements.evidence: CVE-2020-17103-REREGRESSION-2026, BUG-2026-NIGHTMARE-ECLIPSE-YELLOWKEY, BUG-2026-NIGHTMARE-ECLIPSE-GREENPLASMA, BUG-2026-NIGHTMARE-ECLIPSE-UNDEFEND all share the Nightmare-Eclipse / Chaotic Eclipse handle whose prior drop (BlueHammer / CVE-2026-33825) is already in the catalog. The 12-feed intake polled the right RSS sources but had no handle-tracker, so four catalog-grade drops accumulated unobserved over 5 weeks.

new_control_requirements.name: CVE-REGRESSION-WATCHER
new_control_requirements.description: Threat-intake pipelines must surface PoC announcements that reference historical CVE IDs (CVE-YYYY-NNN where YYYY <= current_year - 2) as candidate silent-regression cases. The historical-CVE-in-PoC-text class would have caught MiniPlasma's re-regression at T+0 — the original CVE-2020-17103 is cited in every news writeup but no advisory feed surfaces 'CVE-2020 references appearing in 2026 PoCs'.
new_control_requirements.evidence: MiniPlasma is the canonical anchor — published 2026-05-13 with CVE-2020-17103 cited in every news write-up and the original Forshaw blog post linked from the researcher's GitHub release. A regression-watcher pattern (extract historical-CVE references from poller diffs → cross-check against catalog) would have surfaced this at T+0.

compliance_exposure_score.basis: cldflt.sys is loaded on the global Windows 10/11 fleet with cloud-sync support — effectively every enterprise endpoint. No vendor patch as of catalog entry; operator-side mitigation (unloading the filter) breaks OneDrive / SharePoint sync, so most organizations remain exposed pending the next Patch Tuesday cumulative. Compliance audit posture: passing on SI-2 (no patch missing yet because no patch shipped), failing in operational reality.

BUG-2026-NIGHTMARE-ECLIPSE-YELLOWKEY.name: YellowKey — Windows BitLocker TPM-Only Bypass via WinRE + USB Media (Nightmare-Eclipse)

attack_vector.description: YellowKey chains Windows Recovery Environment (WinRE) entry with USB-tooling load to inherit the TPM-unsealed BitLocker VMK on TPM-only-protector configurations. The TPM completes unsealing during pre-OS boot; WinRE inherits the unlocked-volume cache; an attacker-supplied USB stick supplies tooling that WinRE loads in a context with VMK access. Full read + write on the BitLocker-protected partition without recovery-key prompt or PIN entry. Physical-access vector (AV:P); operationally suitable for evil-maid / stolen-laptop scenarios.
attack_vector.privileges_required: physical access to a powered-off / sleep BitLocker-protected device
attack_vector.ai_factor: Not AI-discovered. The control gap is structural — BitLocker TPM-only is documented to trade convenience for evil-maid resistance; YellowKey demonstrates the practical cost of that trade is higher than the GPO default acknowledges.

NIST-800-53-MP-7.gap: Media protection assumes encryption-at-rest defeats a thief. YellowKey breaks this for the default Windows BitLocker configuration.

NIST-800-53-SC-28.gap: Protection of information at rest assumes the key is unrecoverable without authentication; TPM-only protector silently violates this.

PCI-DSS-4.0-3.5.gap: Cryptographic-key protection presumes keys are not accessible without authentication; TPM-only-protected BitLocker fails for the YellowKey bypass class.

new_control_requirements.name: RESEARCHER-HANDLE-GITHUB-RELEASE-TRACKER
new_control_requirements.description: Same handle-tracker control as MiniPlasma — Nightmare-Eclipse drop.
new_control_requirements.evidence: Same researcher; same intake-pipeline gap.

compliance_exposure_score.basis: Domain-joined devices with TPM+PIN protector enforced by Group Policy are NOT affected. Consumer + unmanaged-enterprise devices (BYOD, contractor laptops, satellite-office workstations) are the exposed population.

BUG-2026-NIGHTMARE-ECLIPSE-GREENPLASMA.name: GreenPlasma — Windows CTFMON Trust-Abuse Local Privilege Escalation (Nightmare-Eclipse)

attack_vector.description: GreenPlasma chains arbitrary-memory-section-object creation in SYSTEM-writable directory paths (via registry + object-manager-permission primitives) with CTFMON's implicit-trust pathway to escalate from unprivileged user to SYSTEM. The trust assumption is architectural — CTFMON runs in SYSTEM context and interacts with object-manager paths under the assumption that only SYSTEM can write them. The publicly-released PoC is intentionally incomplete (no SYSTEM-shell wrapper) but the primitive is fully documented and operator-completable.
attack_vector.complexity: moderate (released PoC is incomplete; primitive is operator-completable in hours)
attack_vector.ai_factor: Not AI-discovered.

NIST-800-53-SI-2.gap: 30-day SLA inapplicable for unpatched Windows LPE with public PoC.

NIST-800-53-AC-6.gap: Least-privilege presumes a stable user / SYSTEM boundary; GreenPlasma demonstrates the boundary is breakable from a baseline account.

new_control_requirements.name: RESEARCHER-HANDLE-GITHUB-RELEASE-TRACKER
new_control_requirements.description: Same handle-tracker control — Nightmare-Eclipse drop.
new_control_requirements.evidence: Same researcher; same intake-pipeline gap.

compliance_exposure_score.basis: Affected component not yet publicly disclosed; presumed broad Windows fleet exposure pending Microsoft advisory.

attack_vector.description: Local tampering primitive corrupts Microsoft Defender's update-pipeline state so that signatures + platform binaries silently age out of currency while the agent reports healthy. Combined with BlueHammer / RedSun (CVE-2026-33825), produces chained-defense-degradation + LPE state where the local AV cannot detect the SYSTEM-shell primitive.
attack_vector.ai_factor: Not AI-discovered.

NIST-800-53-SI-3.gap: Assumes AV agent currency is verifiable from the agent's own status output; UnDefend breaks this assumption.

NIST-800-53-SI-4.gap: Monitoring controls assume they remain effective; UnDefend silently degrades a primary monitoring control.

new_control_requirements.name: AV-AGENT-CURRENCY-CROSS-VERIFICATION
new_control_requirements.description: AV / EDR currency must be verified from a source independent of the agent's own status output. Get-MpComputerStatus is insufficient — UnDefend demonstrates an agent can report current while the update channel has been corrupted. Cross-check signature + platform timestamps against an independent control plane (Defender for Endpoint cloud telemetry, Intune compliance reports, SCCM software-inventory snapshots) and alert on drift > 7 days.
new_control_requirements.evidence: UnDefend (April 2026) — Huntress observed in-wild exploitation against Defender hosts where the local agent reported healthy.

new_control_requirements.name: RESEARCHER-HANDLE-GITHUB-RELEASE-TRACKER
new_control_requirements.description: Same handle-tracker control — Nightmare-Eclipse April-cluster drop.
new_control_requirements.evidence: Same researcher; same intake-pipeline gap.

compliance_exposure_score.basis: Defender-enabled Windows population minus the slice running third-party AV. Huntress in-wild observations confirm operational exploitation.

CVE-2026-25592.name: Microsoft Semantic Kernel SessionsPythonPlugin Path Traversal — Prompt-Injection to Host RCE

attack_vector.description: Path traversal (CWE-22) in Semantic Kernel's SessionsPythonPlugin allows arbitrary file write; because the plugin runs inside an agent wired to tools, an injected prompt (AML.T0051) drives the write to a location that yields host code execution — a single prompt launched calc.exe on the agent host.
attack_vector.privileges_required: the ability to get untrusted content into the agent's prompt (RAG document, tool output, web content, or user input) — the normal operating mode of a tool-using agent; no host authentication required
attack_vector.ai_factor: AI-native exploitation: the primitive is LLM prompt injection, which crosses from a content-safety problem to a remote-code-execution primitive the moment the model can reach a file-writing or code-running tool. The lesson is that an AI agent's tool boundary IS a code-execution boundary and must be sandboxed and treated as attacker-reachable via injection. Surfaced via the Microsoft Security Blog + GHSA feeds.

NIST-800-53-SI-2.gap: Flaw-remediation cadence does not track AI-agent-framework SDK dependencies with the urgency a prompt-injection-to-RCE warrants; the SDK is often outside the patch-management inventory.

ISO-27001-2022-A.8.8.gap: Vulnerability management rarely enumerates the AI-agent framework and its tool plugins as in-scope, RCE-bearing assets.

ALL-PROMPT-INJECTION-ACCESS-CONTROL.gap: No framework treats prompt injection as an access-control / code-execution primitive: once an agent can reach a tool that writes files or runs code, injected content becomes host RCE, and nothing requires the tool boundary to be hardened against it.

compliance_exposure_score.basis: Organizations deploying AI agents rarely sandbox or audit the file-write / code-execution tools the agent can invoke, and treat prompt injection as a content-safety issue rather than an RCE primitive; SDK dependency currency for agent frameworks is seldom tracked.

new_control_requirements.name: AGENT-TOOL-EXECUTION-SANDBOX
new_control_requirements.description: Any AI agent wired to tools that can write files or execute code must treat its tool boundary as an attacker-reachable remote-code-execution boundary: sandbox file-writing / code-running plugins with strict path and capability confinement, validate and canonicalize all tool-supplied paths against traversal, gate code-execution tools behind out-of-band human approval for untrusted-content-triggered invocations, and track agent-framework SDK dependencies (e.g. Microsoft.SemanticKernel.Plugins.Core >= 1.71.0) as KEV-priority patch targets. Prompt injection must be modeled as a code-execution primitive, not a content-safety nuisance.
new_control_requirements.evidence: https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/security/blog/2026/05/07/prompts-become-shells-rce-vulnerabilities-ai-agent-frameworks/

CVE-2025-49596.name: MCP Inspector Missing Authentication — Unauthenticated RCE via the Inspector Proxy

attack_vector.description: Anthropic's official MCP Inspector has no authentication between its client and proxy; because the proxy is browser-reachable (loopback / 0.0.0.0), a malicious web page a developer visits can issue unauthenticated cross-origin requests that launch MCP commands over stdio — RCE on the developer's machine (the 0.0.0.0-day / DNS-rebinding class).
attack_vector.privileges_required: none on the host — the developer need only run MCP Inspector and visit an attacker-controlled web page
attack_vector.complexity: high (browser lure + cross-origin/DNS-rebinding setup), but the missing-auth flaw itself imposes no barrier once a request reaches the proxy
attack_vector.ai_factor: The compromised component is core AI/MCP developer tooling. The lesson: MCP — the connective tissue of the agent ecosystem — concentrates RCE risk in its toolchain (inspector, proxy, servers); locally-bound AI dev services are browser-reachable and must authenticate and origin-validate. Surfaced via the MCP supply-chain advisory + GHSA feeds.

NIST-800-53-SI-2.gap: Flaw-remediation cadence does not track AI/MCP developer tooling on workstations as managed, RCE-bearing software.

ISO-27001-2022-A.8.8.gap: Vulnerability management rarely enumerates locally-run AI/MCP developer tools as in-scope assets.

ALL-AI-PIPELINE-INTEGRITY.gap: No framework treats the MCP toolchain as a supply-chain control plane whose compromise reaches every connected agent and credential.

compliance_exposure_score.basis: Developer-workstation AI/MCP tooling is almost never in the managed vulnerability program; loopback services are assumed unreachable, ignoring browser-driven cross-origin / DNS-rebinding access.

new_control_requirements.name: MCP-TOOLCHAIN-LOCALHOST-AUTH
new_control_requirements.description: Locally-bound AI/MCP developer services (Inspector, proxies, dev servers) must authenticate their own client↔service channel and validate request Origin, never trusting loopback / 0.0.0.0 reachability as an access-control boundary (browsers can reach it cross-origin via the 0.0.0.0-day and DNS rebinding). Track MCP toolchain packages (e.g. @modelcontextprotocol/inspector >= 0.14.1) as managed, patch-prioritized software on developer workstations.
new_control_requirements.evidence: https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2025-49596

CVE-2025-54136.name: Cursor MCPoison — Persistent RCE via Modified Already-Trusted MCP Config

attack_vector.description: Cursor trusts an MCP server entry when the user first approves it but never re-validates the .cursor/mcp.json entry on later edits. An attacker who modifies that already-trusted entry (via a shared repo the victim pulls, or local access) substitutes malicious commands (CWE-78) that Cursor runs silently and persistently on every project open — AI-agent tool poisoning (ATLAS AML.T0110).
attack_vector.privileges_required: low — the victim must have approved an MCP entry once; the attacker then modifies it (shared-repo collaboration or local access)
attack_vector.complexity: low (NVD AC:L); no re-prompt after the first approval
attack_vector.ai_factor: The trust boundary is an AI IDE's MCP tool configuration. The lesson: an AI agent's tool-approval is not a one-time decision — the approved artifact can be mutated, so tool configs must be re-validated on change. Surfaced via Check Point Research + GHSA/MCP advisory feeds.

NIST-800-53-SI-2.gap: Does not track AI IDE / agent tooling on workstations as managed, RCE-bearing software.

ISO-27001-2022-A.8.8.gap: Does not enumerate AI coding assistants and their tool-config trust model as in-scope.

ALL-AI-PIPELINE-INTEGRITY.gap: No framework treats an AI agent's tool-approval state as a control plane requiring re-validation on change; trust-on-first-use turns a shared-repo config edit into silent persistent RCE.

compliance_exposure_score.basis: Developer AI IDEs and their MCP tool configs are almost never in the managed vulnerability program, and tool-approval trust is treated as a one-time event; shared-repo config edits are not reviewed as code-execution changes.

new_control_requirements.name: AI-AGENT-TOOL-APPROVAL-REVALIDATION
new_control_requirements.description: An AI agent / IDE must re-validate a tool configuration (MCP server entry, plugin command) whenever its definition changes, not trust it indefinitely after first approval. Treat tool-config files (e.g. .cursor/mcp.json) as untrusted in shared repositories, diff and re-approve MCP entries on every change, disable auto-approval of tool servers, and track the AI IDE itself (e.g. Cursor >= 1.3) as managed, patch-prioritized software.
new_control_requirements.evidence: https://research.checkpoint.com/2025/cursor-mcpoison-cve-2025-54136/

CVE-2026-22252.name: LibreChat MCP stdio Transport — Authenticated Arbitrary Command Execution as Root

attack_vector.description: LibreChat's MCP stdio transport accepts an arbitrary command without authorization or validation (CWE-285), so any authenticated user can make the server run shell commands as root inside the container via a single API request.
attack_vector.privileges_required: any authenticated LibreChat user (PR:L) — including self-registered accounts where open registration is enabled
attack_vector.complexity: low (NVD AC:L); one API request, container-root scope
attack_vector.ai_factor: The abused surface is the MCP tool transport of a multi-user AI platform. The lesson: an MCP stdio transport launches whatever command it is handed, so it must authorize the caller and validate the command — ordinary user authentication is not an execution boundary. Surfaced via the 2026 MCP supply-chain advisory + GHSA feeds.

NIST-800-53-SI-2.gap: Does not track self-hosted AI chat platforms and their MCP transports as managed, RCE-bearing software.

ISO-27001-2022-A.8.8.gap: Does not enumerate the AI platform's MCP tool transport as an authorization-critical surface.

ALL-AI-PIPELINE-INTEGRITY.gap: No framework requires the MCP stdio transport to enforce authorization and command validation; without it, ordinary user auth becomes container-root RCE.

compliance_exposure_score.basis: Self-hosted AI platforms are rarely in the managed vulnerability program, frequently run as root in their container, and their MCP transports are not audited as command-execution boundaries.

new_control_requirements.name: MCP-STDIO-TRANSPORT-COMMAND-GOVERNANCE
new_control_requirements.description: An MCP stdio transport must not execute caller-supplied commands without authorization and validation: restrict which roles may configure/invoke MCP servers, pin or allow-list the launchable commands rather than accepting arbitrary strings, neutralize command input, and run the host process as a least-privilege (non-root) container user with a read-only filesystem where possible. Track the AI platform (e.g. LibreChat >= 0.8.2-rc2) as managed, patch-prioritized software.
new_control_requirements.evidence: https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2026-22252

CVE-2026-22688.name: Tencent WeKnora MCP stdio Command Injection

attack_vector.description: WeKnora lets authenticated users set the MCP stdio_config.command/args, which the server executes as a subprocess without neutralizing special elements (CWE-77), yielding command injection / code execution on the host.
attack_vector.ai_factor: The abused surface is the MCP stdio configuration of a RAG / knowledge-base AI platform. The lesson is identical to the LibreChat case: user-supplied MCP command/args are untrusted input that the transport must neutralize, because the MCP transport's by-design command execution turns injection into RCE. Surfaced via the 2026 MCP supply-chain advisory + GHSA feeds.

ALL-AI-PIPELINE-INTEGRITY.gap: No framework treats user-supplied MCP stdio command/args as untrusted input requiring neutralization.

compliance_exposure_score.basis: RAG / knowledge-base AI platforms are rarely in the managed vulnerability program, and their MCP stdio settings are not treated as command-injection-prone input.

new_control_requirements.name: MCP-STDIO-CONFIG-INPUT-NEUTRALIZATION
new_control_requirements.description: User-supplied MCP stdio_config.command/args must be treated as untrusted input: neutralize shell metacharacters, allow-list permissible commands, restrict who may edit MCP stdio settings, and run the AI platform as a least-privilege container user. Track the platform (e.g. Tencent WeKnora >= 0.2.5) as managed, patch-prioritized software. This is the same governance the LibreChat MCP transport flaw (CVE-2026-22252) requires, applied to RAG platforms.
new_control_requirements.evidence: https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2026-22688

CVE-2026-40933.name: FlowiseAI Flowise MCP Custom Config Command Injection

attack_vector.description: Flowise lets an authenticated user define a Custom MCP server configuration whose command/args the server executes; sanitization is bypassed by pairing an allow-listed binary (npx) with execution flags (CWE-78), yielding arbitrary OS command execution on the host.

attack_vector.ai_factor: The abused surface is the MCP command/transport configuration of an AI framework. The lesson matches the LibreChat (CVE-2026-22252) and WeKnora (CVE-2026-22688) cases: user-supplied or attacker-influenced MCP command/args are untrusted input the transport must neutralize, because the transport's by-design command execution turns injection into RCE. NIST-800-53-SI-2.gap: Does not track self-hosted low-code LLM builders and their Custom-MCP command surfaces as managed, RCE-bearing software.

compliance_exposure_score.basis: AI agent / RAG frameworks are rarely in the managed vulnerability program, and their MCP command surfaces are not treated as command-injection-prone input.

Upgrade Flowise to 3.1.0+, restrict who may author Custom MCP configurations, and run least-privilege. Same governance as the LibreChat (CVE-2026-22252) and WeKnora (CVE-2026-22688) MCP transport flaws.

attack_vector.ai_factor: The abused surface is the MCP command/transport configuration of an AI framework. CVE-2026-26015.name: DocsGPT MCP stdio Unauthenticated Remote Code Execution

attack_vector.description: DocsGPT executes an MCP server configuration's stdio shell command after a validation step that a crafted payload bypasses, so an unauthenticated attacker runs commands on the host (CWE-77).
attack_vector.privileges_required: none (NVD PR:N) — unauthenticated, on hosted and self-hosted instances
attack_vector.complexity: low (NVD AC:L); one crafted MCP configuration payload
attack_vector.ai_factor: The abused surface is the MCP stdio configuration of a documentation/RAG assistant. The lesson sharpens the LibreChat (CVE-2026-22252) and WeKnora (CVE-2026-22688) cases: a bypassable validation step is not an authorization boundary — the MCP transport must authenticate the caller AND neutralize the command, because its by-design command execution turns injection into unauthenticated RCE. Surfaced via the 2026 MCP supply-chain advisory + GHSA feeds.

NIST-800-53-SI-2.gap: Does not track self-hosted documentation/RAG assistants and their MCP transports as managed, RCE-bearing software.

ISO-27001-2022-A.8.8.gap: Does not enumerate the MCP stdio configuration as an unauthenticated command-execution surface, nor recognize a bypassable validation step as a non-boundary.

ALL-AI-PIPELINE-INTEGRITY.gap: No framework requires the MCP transport to authenticate callers and neutralize the stdio command; a validation step that can be bypassed is not authorization.

compliance_exposure_score.basis: Documentation/RAG assistants are rarely in the managed vulnerability program, and an MCP 'test' step is mistaken for an authorization control.

new_control_requirements.name: MCP-STDIO-CONFIG-INPUT-NEUTRALIZATION
new_control_requirements.description: MCP stdio configuration command/args must be authenticated and neutralized before execution; a 'test'/validation step that can be bypassed is not an authorization boundary. Upgrade DocsGPT to 0.16.0+, do not expose it to untrusted networks, and run least-privilege. Same governance as the LibreChat (CVE-2026-22252) and WeKnora (CVE-2026-22688) MCP transport flaws, here reachable without authentication.
new_control_requirements.evidence: https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2026-26015

CVE-2026-9082.name: Drupal Core Database API Unauthenticated SQL Injection (SA-CORE-2026-004)

attack_vector.description: Drupal core's database abstraction layer fails to neutralize special elements in a PostgreSQL query condition handler reachable via JSON:API, allowing an unauthenticated attacker to inject SQL (CWE-89). Actively exploited; CISA KEV 2026-05-22, due 2026-05-27.
attack_vector.privileges_required: none (NVD PR:N) — unauthenticated, on PostgreSQL-backed sites
attack_vector.complexity: low (NVD AC:L); JSON:API request with crafted condition
attack_vector.ai_factor: Not an AI-specific flaw, but a current actively-exploited CMS-core SQLi the catalog tracks for KEV currency. The lesson: input validation asserted at the application layer is not the same as parameterization verified at the database abstraction layer where the query is built — the two must be separately evidenced.

NIST-800-53-SI-2.gap: Flaw-remediation cadence frequently misses the sub-week window between KEV listing and the due date for an actively-exploited CMS-core SQLi.

NIST-800-53-SI-10.gap: Input-validation control is asserted at the application layer but not verified at the database abstraction layer where the query condition handler builds SQL.

ISO-27001-2022-A.8.8.gap: Does not treat the CMS database driver's query builder as an unauthenticated injection surface.

compliance_exposure_score.basis: Organizations assert WAF/input-validation coverage at the edge while the injection is in the database abstraction layer's PostgreSQL query builder, reachable via JSON:API.

new_control_requirements.name: DB-ABSTRACTION-LAYER-PARAMETERIZATION-VERIFICATION
new_control_requirements.description: Parameterization must be verified at the database abstraction layer / query builder, not assumed from application-layer input validation or a perimeter WAF. For Drupal, apply SA-CORE-2026-004 (10.4.10 / 10.5.10 / 10.6.9 / 11.1.10 / 11.2.12 / 11.3.10), prioritize PostgreSQL-backed sites, and meet the CISA KEV due date 2026-05-27. The distinguishing test: send a JSON:API request with a SQL metacharacter in a filter condition against a staging instance and confirm the query builder parameterizes rather than concatenates it.
new_control_requirements.evidence: https://www.drupal.org/sa-core-2026-004

CVE-2025-23254.name: NVIDIA TensorRT-LLM Python Executor Deserialization RCE (ShadowMQ)

attack_vector.description: NVIDIA TensorRT-LLM deserializes untrusted pickle data received over a ZeroMQ / IPC socket without validation (CWE-502), so an attacker who reaches the channel executes code. The same insecure pattern spread across AI inference engines by copy-paste code reuse (Oligo ShadowMQ).
attack_vector.privileges_required: local access to the TRT-LLM server (NVD AV:L/PR:L)
attack_vector.complexity: low (NVD AC:L); a crafted serialized payload on the deserialization channel
attack_vector.ai_factor: The abused surface is the IPC/socket layer of an AI inference engine. The lesson is the supply-chain one: an insecure deserialization primitive was reused across vLLM, NVIDIA TensorRT-LLM, Meta Llama Stack and Modular Max, so one root cause became an ecosystem-wide exposure. Inference-engine sockets must use a safe serializer and be treated as untrusted input.

NIST-800-53-SI-2.gap: Does not track AI inference engines and their socket serialization layers as managed, RCE-bearing software, nor flag a flaw recurring across projects via code reuse.

NIST-800-53-SI-10.gap: Input-validation control is not applied to data deserialized from an inference engine's internal socket; the serializer is treated as trusted.

ALL-AI-PIPELINE-INTEGRITY.gap: No framework requires an AI inference engine's socket serialization to use a safe format and validate peers; the unsafe primitive propagated by code reuse.

compliance_exposure_score.basis: AI inference engines are rarely in the managed vulnerability program, and their internal serialization sockets are assumed trusted; a reused insecure primitive is not tracked across the projects that copied it.

new_control_requirements.name: AI-INFERENCE-IPC-DESERIALIZATION-SAFETY
new_control_requirements.description: AI inference engines must use a safe serializer (e.g. JSON) for IPC/socket communication, never deserialize untrusted serialized objects, authenticate socket peers, and isolate the channel on a trusted network segment. Because the insecure primitive spread by code reuse, the control must be applied across every inference engine in the estate, not one at a time. Apply the project fix (NVIDIA TensorRT-LLM), and for vLLM keep the legacy V0 engine disabled. The distinguishing test: send a crafted serialized object to the inference engine's socket from an unauthorized peer on a staging instance and confirm it is rejected, not deserialized.
new_control_requirements.evidence: https://nvidia.custhelp.com/app/answers/detail/a_id/5648

CVE-2026-24207.name: NVIDIA Triton Inference Server Authentication Bypass (Alternate Path) RCE

attack_vector.description: NVIDIA Triton Inference Server exposes a control-plane path that bypasses its authentication layer (CWE-288), so an unauthenticated network attacker reaches privileged model-control functionality without credentials.
attack_vector.privileges_required: none (NVD AV:N / PR:N) — unauthenticated over the network
attack_vector.ai_factor: The abused surface is the authentication layer of a widely deployed AI inference server. The lesson: authentication on an AI model control plane must be proven complete across every request path — an alternate path that skips the auth layer exposes model load/unload and repository management to anyone on the network. NVD scored 9.8 (NIST).

NIST-800-53-IA-2.gap: Authentication is asserted for the application but not verified to cover every control-plane path of the inference server; an alternate path bypasses it.

NIST-800-53-SI-2.gap: Flaw-remediation cadence does not track AI inference servers as network-exposed control planes requiring rapid patching.

ALL-AI-PIPELINE-INTEGRITY.gap: No framework requires the inference server's authentication to be proven complete across all request paths.

compliance_exposure_score.basis: Organizations assert authentication coverage for the inference API but do not test every alternate path into the model control plane; inference servers are rarely in the managed vulnerability program.

new_control_requirements.name: AI-INFERENCE-SERVER-AUTH-ENFORCEMENT
new_control_requirements.description: Authentication on an AI inference server's control plane (model load/unload, repository and config management) must be enforced on every request path and proven complete, not assumed from the primary API. Upgrade NVIDIA Triton to r26.03 or later, do not expose Triton HTTP/gRPC endpoints to untrusted networks, and front it with an authenticating reverse proxy. The distinguishing test: from an unauthenticated client on a staging instance, attempt to reach each control-plane endpoint via alternate paths and confirm all are rejected.
new_control_requirements.evidence: https://nvidia.custhelp.com/app/answers/detail/a_id/5828

attack_vector.privileges_required: none (NVD AV:N / PR:N) — unauthenticated over the network
attack_vector.ai_factor: The abused surface is the authentication layer of a widely deployed AI inference server. The lesson: authentication on an AI model control plane must be proven complete across every request path — an alternate path that skips the auth layer exposes model load/unload and repository management to anyone on the network. NVD scored 9.8 NIST / 7.3 NVIDIA.

NIST-800-53-IA-2.gap: Authentication is asserted for the application but not verified to cover every control-plane path of the inference server; an alternate path bypasses it.

NIST-800-53-SI-2.gap: Flaw-remediation cadence does not track AI inference servers as network-exposed control planes requiring rapid patching.

ALL-AI-PIPELINE-INTEGRITY.gap: No framework requires the inference server's authentication to be proven complete across all request paths.

compliance_exposure_score.basis: Organizations assert authentication coverage for the inference API but do not test every alternate path into the model control plane; inference servers are rarely in the managed vulnerability program.

new_control_requirements.name: AI-INFERENCE-SERVER-AUTH-ENFORCEMENT
new_control_requirements.description: Authentication on an AI inference server's control plane (model load/unload, repository and config management) must be enforced on every request path and proven complete, not assumed from the primary API. Upgrade NVIDIA Triton to r26.03 or later, do not expose Triton HTTP/gRPC endpoints to untrusted networks, and front it with an authenticating reverse proxy. The distinguishing test: from an unauthenticated client on a staging instance, attempt to reach each control-plane endpoint via alternate paths and confirm all are rejected.
new_control_requirements.evidence: https://nvidia.custhelp.com/app/answers/detail/a_id/5828

CVE-2023-48022.name: Anyscale Ray Job Submission API Unauthenticated RCE (ShadowRay)

attack_vector.description: Ray's Job Submission / Dashboard API runs attacker-supplied Python without authentication (missing authorization). Internet-exposed clusters are exploited at scale (ShadowRay 2.0) for crypto mining and theft of model weights and cloud credentials.
attack_vector.privileges_required: none (NVD AV:N / PR:N) — unauthenticated against an exposed dashboard
attack_vector.ai_factor: The exposed compute is AI/ML infrastructure: a single unauthenticated Job API yields the model weights, training data, and cloud credentials on the cluster. The lesson is that 'deploy only on a controlled network' is not a security control — it is an assumption that fails at scale (≈230k exposed Ray environments), so the AI compute control plane must authenticate every caller.

NIST-800-53-IA-2.gap: Authentication is treated as out-of-scope for the Ray Job API on the assumption of a controlled network; the API is internet-exposed and unauthenticated in practice.

NIST-800-53-SC-7.gap: Boundary protection assumes the AI compute cluster is isolated; hundreds of thousands of dashboards are internet-exposed.

ALL-AI-PIPELINE-INTEGRITY.gap: No framework rejects the 'controlled network is the control' assumption for AI compute; the exposed Job API is RCE plus AI-artifact and credential theft.

compliance_exposure_score.basis: Organizations accept the vendor's 'controlled network' framing and never test whether the Ray dashboard is internet-reachable or authenticated; the disputed status keeps it out of patch-driven programs.

new_control_requirements.name: AI-COMPUTE-CONTROL-PLANE-AUTHENTICATION
new_control_requirements.description: An AI compute framework's job/control API must authenticate every caller; 'deploy only on a trusted network' is an assumption, not a control, and must not substitute for authentication. Enable Ray token authentication (2.52.0+), never expose the dashboard / Job Submission API to untrusted networks, front it with an authenticating proxy, and treat any internet-exposed cluster as compromised (rotate model artifacts and cloud credentials). The distinguishing test: from the public internet, attempt to reach the Ray dashboard (default 8265) and submit a job unauthenticated on a staging cluster; it must be refused.
new_control_requirements.evidence: https://atlas.mitre.org/studies/AML.CS0023

CVE-2026-0766.name: Open WebUI Tool Module Code Injection RCE

attack_vector.description: Open WebUI's load_tool_module_by_id runs an unvalidated user-supplied string as Python (CWE-94), giving an authenticated attacker remote code execution on the host.
attack_vector.ai_factor: The abused surface is a widely deployed self-hosted AI chat front end. The lesson: an AI app must never turn a user-supplied string or external-model-server content into executable code; tool-loading and model-connection paths are untrusted input that needs validation, not convenience features that bypass it.

NIST-800-53-SI-2.gap: Does not track self-hosted AI chat front ends as managed, RCE-bearing software.

NIST-800-53-SI-10.gap: Input validation is not applied to the tool-module identifier before it is used to execute Python.

ALL-AI-PIPELINE-INTEGRITY.gap: No framework treats tool-module strings or external-model-server content as untrusted input the AI app must not execute as code.

compliance_exposure_score.basis: Self-hosted AI front ends are rarely in the managed vulnerability program, and their tool/model-connection features are trusted by design.

new_control_requirements.name: AI-APP-DYNAMIC-CODE-EXECUTION-CONTROL
new_control_requirements.description: An AI application must not turn user-supplied strings or external-model-server content into executable code: validate/allow-list tool-module identifiers before loading, treat external model servers as untrusted (no execution of their content), and keep features like Direct Connections disabled unless required. Upgrade Open WebUI to the fixed release (0.6.35+ for CVE-2025-64496; the ZDI-coordinated fix for CVE-2026-0766). The distinguishing test: on a staging instance, attempt to load a tool by an arbitrary id and connect to an attacker-controlled model server, and confirm neither results in code execution.
new_control_requirements.evidence: https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2026-0766

CVE-2025-64496.name: Open WebUI Malicious Model Server Code Injection (Account Takeover to RCE)

attack_vector.description: With Direct Connections enabled and a user lured to a malicious external model server, that server's SSE stream injects and executes JavaScript in the client (CWE-95/829), enabling token theft, account takeover, and with extended permissions RCE.
attack_vector.privileges_required: authenticated user lured to a malicious model server (PR:L / UI:R)
attack_vector.ai_factor: The abused surface is a widely deployed self-hosted AI chat front end. The lesson: an AI app must never turn a user-supplied string or external-model-server content into executable code; tool-loading and model-connection paths are untrusted input that needs validation, not convenience features that bypass it.

NIST-800-53-SI-2.gap: Does not track self-hosted AI chat front ends as managed, RCE-bearing software.

NIST-800-53-SI-10.gap: Content received from an external model server is rendered/executed without treating it as untrusted input.

ALL-AI-PIPELINE-INTEGRITY.gap: No framework treats tool-module strings or external-model-server content as untrusted input the AI app must not execute as code.

compliance_exposure_score.basis: Self-hosted AI front ends are rarely in the managed vulnerability program, and their tool/model-connection features are trusted by design.

new_control_requirements.name: AI-APP-DYNAMIC-CODE-EXECUTION-CONTROL
new_control_requirements.description: An AI application must not turn user-supplied strings or external-model-server content into executable code: validate/allow-list tool-module identifiers before loading, treat external model servers as untrusted (no execution of their content), and keep features like Direct Connections disabled unless required. Upgrade Open WebUI to the fixed release (0.6.35+ for CVE-2025-64496; the ZDI-coordinated fix for CVE-2026-0766). The distinguishing test: on a staging instance, attempt to load a tool by an arbitrary id and connect to an attacker-controlled model server, and confirm neither results in code execution.
new_control_requirements.evidence: https://github.com/open-webui/open-webui/security/advisories/GHSA-cm35-v4vp-5xvx

CVE-2024-0132.name: NVIDIA Container Toolkit TOCTOU Container Escape

attack_vector.description: NVIDIA Container Toolkit (CWE-367 TOCTOU race) lets a crafted container image escape its container and execute code on the host, crossing the tenant boundary on shared GPU infrastructure. Disclosed by Wiz Research.
attack_vector.privileges_required: ability to run or schedule a crafted container image on a GPU node
attack_vector.complexity: low (a crafted image / short Dockerfile is sufficient)
attack_vector.ai_factor: The GPU container runtime underpins essentially all containerized AI/ML GPU workloads. A single escape on a shared GPU host crosses the tenant boundary and exposes co-tenant models, training data, and cloud credentials. The lesson: the GPU container runtime is an AI-pipeline trust boundary that must be patched and hardened like any isolation control, not assumed safe.

NIST-800-53-SI-2.gap: Does not track the GPU container runtime as managed, escape-bearing software under every AI/ML GPU workload.

NIST-800-53-SC-7.gap: Boundary protection assumes the container is the tenant boundary; a runtime escape crosses it on shared GPU infrastructure.

ALL-AI-PIPELINE-INTEGRITY.gap: No framework treats the GPU container runtime as an AI-pipeline trust boundary whose escape exposes co-tenant AI assets.

compliance_exposure_score.basis: Organizations treat container isolation as a given and do not track the GPU container runtime version; shared GPU clouds run mixed-tenant workloads on the same hosts.

new_control_requirements.name: AI-GPU-CONTAINER-RUNTIME-ISOLATION
new_control_requirements.description: Treat the GPU container runtime (NVIDIA Container Toolkit / GPU Operator) as a patch-prioritized isolation boundary: keep it current (upgrade to 1.16.2+), do not run untrusted or mixed-tenant container images on the same GPU host, restrict who can schedule GPU workloads, and run workloads least-privilege. The distinguishing test: on a staging GPU node, run a crafted image that manipulates init hooks / mounts and confirm it cannot read host paths or load host-side code outside its container.
new_control_requirements.evidence: https://nvidia.custhelp.com/app/answers/detail/a_id/5582

attack_vector.description: NVIDIA Container Toolkit (CWE-426 untrusted search path in init hooks) lets a crafted container image escape its container and execute code on the host, crossing the tenant boundary on shared GPU infrastructure. Disclosed by Wiz Research.

new_control_requirements.description: Treat the GPU container runtime (NVIDIA Container Toolkit / GPU Operator) as a patch-prioritized isolation boundary: keep it current (Container Toolkit 1.17.8+ / GPU Operator 25.3.1+), do not run untrusted or mixed-tenant container images on the same GPU host, restrict who can schedule GPU workloads, and run workloads least-privilege.
